thread_tweets.text:
for one piece in particular the pop counts are low.

all the best hits have psa pop counts anywhere from 500-2000 or so MAX, on ebay you generally only see a  handful of these cards get listed weekly maybe like 3-6 or so listed per week.

genuinely think the demand is too high and supply is too low and it really doesnt take much to send them so much higher.

its especially prevalent in one piece because the rare cards are actually super rare, whereas pokemon for instance has popcounts up to 20,000+ for the top hits especially in modern sets.

tweets.text:
My brain has been completely fried lately. 

The nonstop dopamine machine of infinite AI vibe coding sessions, endless scrolling timelines on every social media app, switching information streams from Netflix to Disney+ to YouTube to Instagram to video games to ripping TCG packs and pulling gacha machines and trading meme coins and looking for the next hit

It’s destroying my memory, my ability to focus, to concentrate, to stay excited by one project or task for more than a few days let alone a few weeks

Talking to friends and other terminally online people I feel like I am not alone and lots of people are going through similar things lately

I took a break day on Monday to basically reset my brain and it was extremely comforting. Almost no tech devices. Read a physical book, did a physical puzzle, went for a walk, wrote with pen and paper.

I think I (and probably lots of us) need to be so mindful of the dangers of infinite dopamine being shoved down our throats and try and escape and heal from it whenever we can

It feels like I’m steamrolling to early dementia otherwise 🙃
tweets.full_text:
My brain has been completely fried lately. 

The nonstop dopamine machine of infinite AI vibe coding sessions, endless scrolling timelines on every social media app, switching information streams from Netflix to Disney+ to YouTube to Instagram to video games to ripping TCG packs and pulling gacha machines and trading meme coins and looking for the next hit

It’s destroying my memory, my ability to focus, to concentrate, to stay excited by one project or task for more than a few days let alone a few weeks

Talking to friends and other terminally online people I feel like I am not alone and lots of people are going through similar things lately

I took a break day on Monday to basically reset my brain and it was extremely comforting. Almost no tech devices. Read a physical book, did a physical puzzle, went for a walk, wrote with pen and paper.

I think I (and probably lots of us) need to be so mindful of the dangers of infinite dopamine being shoved down our throats and try and escape and heal from it whenever we can

It feels like I’m steamrolling to early dementia otherwise 🙃

tweets.text:
It’s the 4th of August 2026.

Last month you signed up to utilise Fable 5 via usage credits because it’s the leading frontier model of Anthropic.

Dario has been telling you how AI is doing your job already and if you don’t start using it then you’re destined for the permanent underclass.

You decided that enough is enough, you’re going to start utilising Fable 5 to automate aspects of your working life to catch up.

You automate your calendar, your meeting notes, your email priorities and response list.

You build a swarm of six sub agents that read everything you need to know about your industry and provide a detailed document for you every morning.

Wow.

You go to fill up gas, no money.

That’s weird? You’re not poor.

You make your way home and load up Fable to review your finances.

It won’t answer for you.

You then look at your token usage meter that you had enabled auto-reload on, it’s full and it hasn’t reloaded?

KNOCK KNOCK.

You think “wtf, I have a doorbell”

KNOCK KNOCK.

Oh what is it?

You go to the front door.

Bailiff’s.

With an invoice for your token usage.

$86700

You go to slam the door on them.

They put their foot on the door, and they show you a photo that they have permission to take valued goods from the property in the name of Dario.

You think out loud “who’s Dario?”

They tell you “Dario Amodei”

“What the hell? That AI guy? He made it sound like I’d lose my job if I didn’t start using Fable”

Yes, well her owns your house now.

“MY HOUSE?”

Yes, you read the terms & conditions when you signed them prior to Fable right?

~~~~

Okay, lmao, enough of this story. I’m just in disbelief that the real permanent underclass is just the same one that currently exists, which is us mere mortals trying to get ourselves into the middle class vs the filthy rich, where we will never be able to afford utilising these frontier models on a regular basis, whilst the giga rich will just use it to make the divide that already exists even bigger. Uhhhhhhhhh.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: Woah the subsidy era is over https://t.co/Sfp7uq8FH5

tweets.text:
I remember in the peak of 2021, The Sandbox was one of the projects that excited me most about the potential of NFTs and "The Metaverse".

While so many other projects have come and gone, it's nice to see that they're still around, and are building tools using the latest AI tech to empower creators within their ecosystem.

Their new AI game engine allows for anyone to build games compatible with The Sandbox, and as a game lover and AI lover, I'm keen to check it out myself and see what I might be able to create.

They're currently in closed alpha, so check out the post below if you are interested in getting access yourself 👇
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
The Sandbox Studio
The AI game engine for the next generation of creators

Apply for early access 👇
https://t.co/tIbW0Tybk7 https://t.co/Cb7x8hA4QC

While so many other projects have come and gone, it's nice to see that they're still around, and are building tools using the latest AI tech to empower creators within their ecosystem.

Their new AI game engine allows for anyone to build games compatible with The Sandbox, and as a game lover and AI lover, I'm keen to check it out myself and see what I might be able to create.

They're currently in closed alpha, so check out the post below if you are interested in getting access yourself 👇

tweets.text:
This is a super exciting release - Claude Fable 5 is the same underlying model as Mythos but with added safeguards. The benchmarks are great and it's SOTA on everything by a margin but I'll add that *qualitatively* also, this is a major-version-bump-deserving step change forward (imo of the same order as Claude 4.5 was in November), peaking especially for long problem-solving sessions on very difficult problems. You can give it a lot more ambitious tasks than what you're used to, the model "gets it" and it will just go, and it's never felt this tempting to stop looking at the code at all (but don't do this in prod!). The model still has quirks that people will run into and the safeguards are configured to be a little too trigger happy for launch, which can hopefully be tuned over time.

I feel a lot of things changing as working software increasingly comes out on a tap. The Jevon's paradox kicks in and I feel my own demand for software growing substantially. You can ask for anything - explainers, visualizers, dashboards, bespoke single-use apps (e.g. a full wandb that is hyper-specific just for your project), you can 10X your test suite, auto-optimize code, run giant research projects with custom HTML for the results, anything! "Free your mind" (Matrix ref). Really looking forward to all the things people build!
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Fable 5 is state-of-the-art on nearly all tested benchmarks, with exceptional performance in software engineering, knowledge work, scientific research, and vision.

The longer and more complex the task, the larger Fable 5’s lead over our other models. https://t.co/DxgSu0KUxh
tweets.full_text:
This is a super exciting release - Claude Fable 5 is the same underlying model as Mythos but with added safeguards. The benchmarks are great and it's SOTA on everything by a margin but I'll add that *qualitatively* also, this is a major-version-bump-deserving step change forward (imo of the same order as Claude 4.5 was in November), peaking especially for long problem-solving sessions on very difficult problems. You can give it a lot more ambitious tasks than what you're used to, the model "gets it" and it will just go, and it's never felt this tempting to stop looking at the code at all (but don't do this in prod!). The model still has quirks that people will run into and the safeguards are configured to be a little too trigger happy for launch, which can hopefully be tuned over time.

I feel a lot of things changing as working software increasingly comes out on a tap. The Jevon's paradox kicks in and I feel my own demand for software growing substantially. You can ask for anything - explainers, visualizers, dashboards, bespoke single-use apps (e.g. a full wandb that is hyper-specific just for your project), you can 10X your test suite, auto-optimize code, run giant research projects with custom HTML for the results, anything! "Free your mind" (Matrix ref). Really looking forward to all the things people build!

tweets.text:
If you’re referencing the recent lawsuits, that’s a typical thing whenever a stock falls 50%. After you hear it once, you’ll start noticing it everywhere.

It doesn’t mean much and it’s usually not important. It’s simply the standard modus operandi: stock drops hard, law firms announce investigations, lawsuits get filed.

tweets.text:
first impression of Fable:

engineermaxxed.

clearly optimized for coding, to the point where it feels autistic on other things.

on a few strategy / reasoning / writing tasks, the responses felt overcomplicated, too detailed, and missing the forest for the trees.

which in a way is fine - not every model needs to be great at everything.

however since it’s engineermaxxed, the obvious gap is using it for security where it’s nerfed

tweets.text:
Claude Fable 5 was just released and shows an enourmous improvement on the Cybersecurity bench (78% Fable vs 40% Opus and 34% GPT).

However this claim is useless to you because prompting it for cybersecurity work will cause it to automatically fall back to Opus 4.8. https://t.co/u0iym0IZBJ
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Releasing a model this capable comes with risks. Without safeguards, Fable 5’s capabilities in areas like cybersecurity could be misused to cause serious damage.

Queries on a narrow range of topics will instead receive a response from our next-most-capable model, Opus 4.8. https://t.co/vJ71vCdkjc

tweets.text:
first 10 hours of using claude fable, mixed reactions - the model's fantastic at most things but unecessarily restrictive in this initial release: 

- this models BURNS through tokens, hit my limit within 2 hrs, had to pay for more usage

- its an amazing world simulator. crushes at building game engines, video games, nails visually stunning graphics unlike prior models

- really annoying usage restrictions prevent me from researching basic science blogs. the sandboxing needs to be loosened

- you can't even analyze anthropic's own system card for fable citing cyber security risks.

- creative writing is amazing (as its name suggests)

-  much less sycophantic / dumb-sounding vs opus.

- basic math exploration defaults to opus 4.8

im hoping anthropic loosens restrictions asap so the public can really test this models capabilities. think it has huge potential.
